{{infobox religious building
 | name = Atago Shrine (Yonezawa City/Toyama)

 | address = [[Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Yonezawa City]], Tooyama Town 2060-11
 | deity = {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|qid=Q1640658|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}; {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|qid=Q386563|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
 | location map = {{Location map|Japan Yamagata Prefecture|label=Atago Shrine (Yonezawa City/Toyama)|float=center|relief=1|width=200|qq=}}
 | coordinates = {{Coord|37.89527777777778|N|140.07472222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
 | creation = Founded in 807 (in the 2nd year of the Daidō era)
 | festival = Held on August 1–2
| location = {{ill|Yonezawa|qid=Q734378|1=Yonezawa, Yamagata}}
 }}
== Overview ==
'''Atago Shrine (Yonezawa City/Toyama)''' (愛宕神社) is a Shinto shrine enshrined atop Atago-yama in Tooyama Town, Yonezawa City, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|qid=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}. It is dedicated to Kagutsuchi and has received patronage from various local lords.
== History ==
According to tradition, it was founded in 807. Over the centuries it has been revered by successive lords such as the Nagai, Date, and Gamo clans.  
== References ==
{{Reflist|qq=}}

* [http://www.city.yonezawa.yamagata.jp/1757.html Official Yonezawa City page]
